content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Stacey Simon 
County Counsel 
Mono County 
South County Offices 
P.O. Box 2415 
Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-065 
Dear Ms. Simon: 
April 13, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Mono County Supervisor Bob 
Gardner regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act 1 (the "Act") and 
Section 1090. Please note that we do not advise on any other area of law, including Public Contract 
Code or common law conflicts of interest. We are also not a finder of fact when rendering advice 
(/11 re Oglesby ( 1975) l FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete 
and accurate. 
As required by Section 1097.1(c)(3), we have forwarded your request to the Attorney 
General's Office and the Mono County District Attorney's Office. We did not receive a written 
response from either entity. (See Section 1097.1 (c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for 
purposes of Section I 090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against 
any individual other than the requester." (Section 1097.l(c)(S).) 
QUESTIONS 
1. Does the Act prohibit Supervisor Gardner from taking part in governmental decisions 
relating to real property owned by Mono County and collectively referred to as "Conway Ranch," 
given that the Supervisor also serves as the uncompensated President of the Board of Directors of 
the Eastern Sierra Land Trust (the "Trust"), a true-exempt 50l(c)(3) nonprofit organization, and that 
Conway Ranch is subject to a conservation easement held by the Trust? 
2. Does Section I 090 prohibit the Supervisor from making or participating in the making of, 
or the County from entering into, contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway Ranch? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references arc to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSIONS 
File No. A-17-065 
Page No. 2 
1. No. The Act does not prohibit the Supervisor from taking part in decisions relating to 
Conway Ranch because he does not have a financial interest in those decisions. 
2. No. Section 1090 does not prohibit the Supervisor from making or participating in the 
making of, or the County from entering into, contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway 
Ranch because the noninterest exception set forth in l09 l.5(a)(8) applies, so long as the 
Supervisor's interest is noted in the County's official records. 
FACTS 
Mono County Supervisor Bob Gardner also serves as the uncompensated President of the 
Board of Directors of the Easter Sierra Land Trust (the "Trust"), a tax-exempt 50 I ( c )(3) nonprofit 
organization. The Trust's purpose is to preserve land in the Eastern Sierra within Mono and Inyo 
Counties for habitat, conservation, and historic preservation. The primary way in which the Trust 
accomplishes this purpose is by acquiring, by purchase or donation, conservation easements over 
real property parcels in the Eastern Sierra within those Counties. These conservation easements 
impose restrictions on covered parcels that preserve habitat, wildlife, or historic values (as specified 
in each easement), and provide remedies (including injunctive relief or damages), which can be 
exercised by the Trust if a parcel subject to a conservation easement is not properly maintained. 
Mono County owns real property comprised of the Conway Ranch and Mattly Ranches, 
collectively referred to as "Conway Ranch," which is subject to a conservation easement held by 
the Trust. The County and the Trust entered into the agreement that created the conservation 
easement applicable to Conway Ranch in 2014. The Conway Ranch conservation easement is 
recorded against the real property and runs with the land. 
You are the Mono County Counsel and the authorized representative of the Supervisor. 
Because the County owns Conway Ranch, it frequently considers governmental decisions that 
affect that property. You have requested advice regarding whether the Supervisor must abstain from 
decisions relating to Conway Ranch, or from making or participating in the making of contracts 
with third parties pertaining to Conway Ranch, including the following: 
• A contract to lease the Conway Ranch for grazing purposes. 
• 
A contract with an aquaculturalist to raise fish in existing trout-rearing facilities on the 
property. 
• 
A contract with an entity or individual to manage and maintain the property. 
• A contract to sell the property subject to the terms of the conservation easement with approval 
by the Trust as a condition to the sale. (The approval cannot be unreasonably withheld.) 


ANALYSIS 
The Act 
File No. A-17-065 
Page No. 3 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in the making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have 
material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests identified in that section. Section 
87103 identifies the following interests: 
• Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or 
more, or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any 
position of management. (Section 87103(a) and ( d).) 
• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(Section 87103(b).) 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the official within 
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made. (Section 87 l03(e).) 
• The official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate family. 
There is no indication that the decisions relating to Conway Ranch implicate any business or 
real property of, gift to, or personal financial effect on, the Supervisor. The Trust is not a source of 
income to the Supervisor because he is not compensated for his service as President of the Board of 
Directors of the Trust. Thus, the Act does not prohibit the Supervisor from taking part in decisions 
relating to Conway Ranch because he does not have a financial interest in those decisions based on 
the facts presented. 
Section 1090 
Section I 090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Section I 090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote or minimal interests, that prevent public officials from 
exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their 
agencies. (Stigall v. City of Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) Under Section 1090, "the prohibited 
act is the making of a contract in which the official has a financial interest." (People v. Honig 
( 1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) The analysis of a financial interest under Section 1090 is separate 
and distinct from that applied under the Act. 


File No. A-17-065 
Page No. 4 
A contract that violates Section l 090 is void, regardless of whether the terms of the contract 
are fair and equitable to all parties. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646-649.) When 
Section 1090 is applicable to one member of a governing body of a public entity, the prohibition 
cannot be avoided by having the interested board member abstain; the entire governing body is 
precluded from entering into the contract. (Id. at pp. 647-649.) 
We employ a six-step analysis to determine whether an official has a disqualifying conflict 
of interest under Section 1090. These steps are: ( 1) does the matter involve a public officer or 
employee; (2) does the matter involve a contract; (3) is the official making or participating in 
making a contract; (4) does the official have a financial interest in the contract; (5) does a remote or 
noninterest exception apply; and, under appropriate facts, (6) does the rule of necessity apply? 
Steps one through three are not at issue here. A public officer is involved because a member 
of the Board of Supervisors is a public officer. Contracts are involved because the County is 
considering entering into contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway Ranch. And because an 
officer is conclusively presumed to participate in the making of his or her agency's contracts when 
the officer is a member of a board or commission that has the power to execute the contract at issue 
(Thomso11, supra, p. 649.), the Supervisor will be presumed to have participated in the making of 
those contracts pertaining to Conway Ranch. 
Steps Four and Five: Does the Supervisor have an interest in the contracts pertaining to 
Conway Ranch, and if so what type of interest? 
The Legislature has created various statutory exceptions to Section 1090's prohibition where 
the interest involved is deemed a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091, or a "noninterest," 
as defined in Section 1091.5. 
If a "noninterest'' is present, the contract may be made without the officer's abstention. ( City 
of Vemo11 v. Central Basin Mun. Water Dist. ( 1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 508, 514-515; 84 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 159-160 (2001).) 
Section I 09 l .5(a)(8) provides that an officer of a public agency shall not be deemed to be 
interested in a contract if the officer's interest in the contract is that of a noncompensated officer of 
a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, to which the body or board has a legal obligation to give 
particular consideration, and provided further that this interest is noted in its official records. 
The Supervisor also serves as the uncompensated President of the Board of Directors of the 
Trust, and the Trust is a tax-exempt 50l(c)(3) nonprofit organization. The County owns Conway 
Ranch, the County and the Trust created the conservation easement applicable to Conway Ranch in 
2014, and the conservation easement is recorded against the property and runs with the land. 
Pursuant to this conservation easement held by the Trust, the Board of Supervisors has a legal 
obligation to give particular consideration to the conservation of Conway Ranch. Therefore, the 
Supervisor has a noninterest in the potential contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway 
Ranch, so long as the Supervisor's interest is noted in the County's official records.2 
1 We do not analyze Step Six because the rule of necessity is not at issue. 


File No. A-17-065 
Page No. 5 
Thus, we conclude that Section I 090 does not prohibit the Supervisor from making or 
participating in the making of, or the County from entering into, contracts with third parties 
pertaining to Conway Ranch because the noninterest exception set forth in Section 1091.5(a)(8) 
applies, so long as the Supervisor's interest is noted in the County's official records. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

Supervisor's interest is noted in the County's official records.

The Act

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does the Act prohibit Supervisor Gardner from taking part in governmental decisions 
relating to real property owned by Mono County and collectively referred to as "Conway Ranch," 
given that the Supervisor also serves as the uncompensated President of the Board of Directors of 
the Eastern Sierra Land Trust (the "Trust"), a true-exempt 50l(c)(3) nonprofit organization, and that 
Conway Ranch is subject to a conservation easement held by the Trust? 
2. Does Section I 090 prohibit the Supervisor from making or participating in the making of, 
or the County from entering into, contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway Ranch?

CONCLUSION: 1. No. The Act does not prohibit the Supervisor from taking part in decisions relating to 
Conway Ranch because he does not have a financial interest in those decisions. 
2. No. Section 1090 does not prohibit the Supervisor from making or participating in the 
making of, or the County from entering into, contracts with third parties pertaining to Conway 
Ranch because the noninterest exception set forth in l09 l.5(a)(8) applies, so long as the 
Supervisor's interest is noted in the County's official records.
